paper_title: No Resource, No Benchmarks, No Problem? Evaluating and Improving LLMs for Code Generation in No-Resource Languages

claims.claim_text: LLMs achieve 0-1% pass@1 on no-resource languages for hard tasks (McEval-Hard)

evidence.description: All four LLMs (GPT-4o, o3-mini, Qwen 2.5 Coder 32B, Qwen 3 32B) scoring 0-1% on Gleam and MoonBit for McEval-Hard
Synthesis: 'close to 0% for hard coding tasks (McEval-Hard)'

claims.claim_text: Syntactic errors dominate in no-resource failures (~2/3 of cases)

location.sentence: roughly two-thirds of the failures are syntactic

evidence.description: o3-mini on Gleam: up to 90% syntactic failures

claims.claim_text: Low-resource languages no longer as challenging as previously reported; 49/60 cases above 50% pass@1

location.sentence: In 49 out of 60 cases (5 languages × 3 benchmarks × 4 models), pass@1 is above 50%

evidence.description: Detailed per-language, per-model pass@1 for low-resource languages

claims.claim_text: Further pre-training outperforms fine-tuning for no-resource languages

location.sentence: Pre-training is the most promising technique

evidence.description: Qwen 2.5 Coder 32B Base pre-trained vs fine-tuned instruct: Gleam McEval-Hard 12.47 vs 3.04; MoonBit 25.86 vs 10.93

claims.claim_text: Pre-training outperforms fine-tuning because it uses more data (all code vs. only paired doc-function)

location.sentence: Indeed, when pre-training, the entire code files...can be used

evidence.description: Pre-training data: 28.3M tokens (Gleam), 13.7M tokens (MoonBit). Fine-tuning data: 3.6M tokens (Gleam), 0.5M tokens (MoonBit)

claims.claim_text: Instruction transferring boosts pass@1 by +12% on average vs. pre-training alone

location.sentence: an average increase across benchmarks, models and languages of 12%

evidence.description: Δ values for all 12 model×language×benchmark combinations

claims.claim_text: Smaller models (8B) + instruction transfer can outperform larger models (32B) fine-tuned

location.sentence: instruction transferring applied to smaller models can result in outperforming larger and more expensive models

evidence.description: Qwen 3 8B+Diff (Gleam HumanEval: 51.88%) vs Qwen 3 32B fine-tuned (Gleam HumanEval: 23.57%) — up to +28% improvement

claims.claim_text: In-context learning helps but is significantly weaker than training-based approaches

location.sentence: training-based techniques...are more effective than in-context learning

evidence.description: All models: fine-tuning/pre-training pass@1 > 5-shot/RAG pass@1 in all configurations
Few-shot reduces syntactic errors by 15.36%, RAG by 8.94%; fine-tuning/pre-training reduce to <20%

claims.claim_text: MoonBit shows higher improvement from techniques than Gleam, possibly due to AI-friendly language design

location.sentence: The higher gain on MoonBit than on Gleam can be explained by two factors

evidence.description: MoonBit consistently shows higher absolute pass@1 and gains from techniques
MoonBit design paper claims 'AI-driven language design' and 'more flexible retrieval-based prompt augmentation'

claims.claim_text: Pre-training base models does not harm instruction following when combined with weight diff transfer

location.sentence: This results in a model that features all instruction-following capabilities of an instruct model

evidence.source: Table VI (Diff column vs. Table IV instruct zero-shot)
evidence.description: Instruction-transferred models outperform or match zero-shot instruct models on no-resource languages

metadata.extraction_method: Manual extraction from arXiv HTML full text
